title: Getting random 403 Forbidden errors on the dashboard
body: I keep getting 403 errors when I try to access the TaskFlow dashboard. It's not every time — sometimes it loads fine, sometimes I get a 403 page. It seems totally random. My permissions haven't changed and other people on my team say they're seeing it too. Started happening a couple days ago.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — intermittent 403s are frustrating, especially when permissions haven't changed.

To help me understand the full picture: can you walk me through what you're typically doing when the 403 hits? For example, are you loading the dashboard fresh after logging in, navigating to it from another page, or does it sometimes fail on a page you already have open (like after a refresh or returning from a different tab)? And when it does work, does anything feel different about those times — time of day, browser, how you got there?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: It's pretty much any time I load the dashboard — fresh login, refreshing the page, doesn't matter. There's no pattern I can see with time of day or anything like that. Sometimes I just hit refresh a couple times and it works on the second or third try, which is why I was wondering if it's some kind of network issue on our end. I don't think it's browser-related since I've seen it in both Chrome and Firefox.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's really helpful — the fact that it happens across browsers and regardless of how you navigate there strongly suggests this isn't on your end.

You mentioned it started a couple of days ago. I'm curious about what was happening around that time: do you know if there were any changes on your side or your team's side — things like a software update, a change in how you connect to the network (VPN, new office, etc.), or anything your IT team rolled out? And on the TaskFlow side, did you notice any maintenance announcements or updates around when it started?

Also, when you and your teammates get the 403, does the error page look like it's coming from TaskFlow itself (styled like the app), or is it a plain/generic error page that might be from a proxy or gateway in front of the app?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body:
Now that you mention it, I think our IT team did do some kind of update or deployment a couple days ago — I remember getting an email about new role changes or something. I was actually just assigned a new role recently, the 'analyst' role, so that might be related? But I'm not sure why it would only fail sometimes.

As for the error page, it looks like a pretty standard 403 page — I think it's from TaskFlow itself but honestly I'm not 100% sure. It's not like a totally blank page or anything.

Oh and I should mention — the teammates who are also seeing this were also assigned the analyst role around the same time as me. I don't think the people with regular admin or editor access are having problems, but I haven't asked everyone.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
This is really clicking into place — the analyst role connection is a strong lead, especially since the people without that role seem unaffected. That said, the intermittent part is interesting: if the analyst role simply didn't have dashboard access, you'd expect it to fail every time, not just sometimes.

So let me ask about the pattern of the failures a bit more closely: when you hit refresh and it works on the second or third try, does it then stay working for a while? Like, can you use the dashboard normally for a stretch before it breaks again? Or is it more like every single page load is a coin flip — it might work, it might not, with no rhyme or reason?

Also, when it does fail, is it just the main dashboard page, or have you noticed 403s on other parts of TaskFlow as well (like specific projects, settings, reports, etc.)?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body:
It's more like a coin flip honestly. Even if it loads fine, the next time I click to another page or refresh it might 403 again. There's no stretch where it just works reliably — it's kind of random each time. I'd say maybe 1 out of every 3 page loads fails, roughly? Could be coincidence but that's what it feels like.

And yeah, it's not just the dashboard — I've gotten 403s on project pages and reports too. Pretty much anywhere I go in the app can hit it.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Intermittent 403 Forbidden errors for users with the new 'analyst' role across all pages","problem":"Users assigned the recently introduced 'analyst' role experience 403 Forbidden errors on approximately 1 in 3 page loads across the entire TaskFlow application (dashboard, projects, reports). The errors are non-deterministic — refreshing may succeed or fail with no pattern. Users with admin or editor roles are unaffected. The issue began immediately after a deployment that introduced the analyst role.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The deployment that introduced the analyst role likely did not propagate consistently across all application instances behind a load balancer, or there is a permission/session caching layer that intermittently serves stale authorization data that does not recognize the analyst role's grants. The coin-flip pattern (~33% failure, no sticky success periods) is characteristic of requests being round-robin distributed across multiple backends where some have correct role-permission mappings and at least one does not.","reproduction_steps":["Assign a test user the 'analyst' role (and no other roles like admin or editor)","Log in as that user and navigate to the dashboard","Refresh the page repeatedly (10-15 times) and observe that some loads return 403 while others succeed","Confirm the same behavior occurs on project pages and reports","Confirm that a user with admin or editor role does not experience any 403 errors under the same conditions"],"environment":"TaskFlow web application, post-deployment from approximately 2 days ago that introduced the 'analyst' role. Affects Chrome and Firefox. Multiple team members affected. Likely multi-instance deployment behind a load balancer.","severity":"high","impact":"All users with the analyst role are unable to use TaskFlow reliably — roughly a third of their page loads fail. This effectively makes the application unusable for the analyst user class, as any navigation or refresh may produce a 403.","recommended_fix":"1. Check whether all application instances/pods have the same version of the role-permission configuration — look for any instance still running the pre-deployment config that doesn't recognize the analyst role. 2. Inspect any permission caching layer (Redis, in-memory cache, CDN) for stale authorization data that predates the analyst role introduction. 3. Verify the analyst role's permission grants in the database/config are complete and cover all application routes (dashboard, projects, reports, etc.). 4. If using rolling deployments, confirm the rollout completed fully and no old replicas are still serving traffic.","proposed_test_case":"Integration test: create a user with only the analyst role, issue 50 sequential authenticated requests to the dashboard endpoint, and assert that all return 200. Run this against each individual backend instance to identify any instance returning 403.","information_gaps":["Exact deployment infrastructure (number of instances, load balancer type, deployment strategy) — this is a server-side detail the reporter would not know","Whether any permission caching layer exists and its TTL configuration","The exact error page content and any request IDs or trace headers that could pinpoint which backend served the 403"]}
